weather_report:
Late morning in Cottesloe arrives under a dappled sky, the sun slipping through torn fields of cloud and laying a cool sheen across the water. The air feels amiable and salt-clean, with visibility crisp despite the gauze above; when the sun punches through, it has some sting to it, so expect a moderate-to-high UV bite through the middle of the day even with the cloud fracturing the light. The pattern looks steady into afternoon: broken cloud, bright intervals, no great threat of rain, and a light sea breeze likely gathering its thoughts after lunch. Altogether, a gentle, good-tempered autumn day.

Dress in easy layers: a breathable shirt, shorts or light trousers, a hat and sunglasses, and a touch of sunscreen if you’ll be out through midday; keep a light jumper handy in case the breeze freshens later. It’s a day for ordinary pleasures done unrushed: a walk along the foreshore while the light is soft, coffee on Napoleon Street with the paper, a small grocery run on foot, or a quiet hour hanging washing that will dry in fits of sun. If the water looks inviting, a brief swim or wade between sunny breaks suits the mood; otherwise, a slow roll on the bike toward Fremantle and back before the afternoon glare asserts itself.
